zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp於大約1435年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾波維其可時，他的父親John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick27歲，母親Margaret de Ferrers23歲。他於1447年1月27日與Elizabeth Stafford在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾阿爾斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1503年1月19日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德布盧爾享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp于大约1435年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾波維其可时，他的父亲John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick27岁，母亲Margaret de Ferrers23岁。他于1447年1月27日与Elizabeth Stafford在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾阿爾斯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1503年1月19日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德布盧爾享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp was born about 1435, in Powick, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick, was 27 and his mother, Margaret de Ferrers, was 23. He married Elizabeth Stafford on 27 January 1447, in Alcester, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 19 January 1503, in Broomhill, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp est né environ 1435, à Powick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Ferrers, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Stafford le 27 janvier 1447, à Alcester, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 19 janvier 1503, à Broomhill, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchampが約1435年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアポウィックで生まれた時，父John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powickは27歳，母Margaret de Ferrersは23歳であった。1447年1月27日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアアルセスターでElizabeth Staffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1503年1月19日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドブルームヒルで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 파윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Ferrers(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1447년 1월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 알스스터에서 Elizabeth Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1503년 1월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 브룸힐에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp родился около 1435 г. в местности поуикк, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Ferrers, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Stafford 27 января 1447 г. в местности алсестир, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 19 января 1503 г. в местности брумхилл, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет.
